perturbation.original_text: DEFINITIONS As used herein, the terms set forth below shall be defined as follows: ENDORSEMENT shall include only the right to use the name, any nickname, initials, autograph, facsimile signature, photograph, portrait, likeness, and/or endorsement of Nantz. ASHWORTH APPAREL shall mean all ASHWORTH(R) brand sportswear apparel contained in the Company's present and future collections (during the Term (as defined below)). ASHWORTH PRODUCTS shall mean, collectively, Ashworth Apparel and Ashworth hats and shoes. DISABILITY shall mean mental or physical illness or condition rendering Nantz incapable of fulfilling the services to be provided by him under this Agreement for a continuous period of at least 60 days. PREMIUM PROGRAM shall mean any traffic builder or other program involving the use of a premium to sell products or services other than Ashworth products and shall include any program primarily designed to attract the consumer to purchase a product or service other than Ashworth Products themselves. CONTRACT YEAR shall mean a period of twelve (12) successive months commencing on any first day of June during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: DEFINITIONS As used herein, the terms set forth below shall be defined as follows: ASHWORTH APPAREL shall mean all ASHWORTH(R) brand sportswear apparel contained in the Company's present and future collections (during the Term (as defined below)). ASHWORTH PRODUCTS shall mean, collectively, Ashworth Apparel and Ashworth hats and shoes. DISABILITY shall mean mental or physical illness or condition rendering Nantz incapable of fulfilling the services to be provided by him under this Agreement for a continuous period of at least 60 days. PREMIUM PROGRAM shall mean any traffic builder or other program involving the use of a premium to sell products or services other than Ashworth products and shall include any program primarily designed to attract the consumer to purchase a product or service other than Ashworth Products themselves. CONTRACT YEAR shall mean a period of twelve (12) successive months commencing on any first day of June during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the definition of 'ENDORSEMENT', the contract lacks clarity regarding the scope of rights granted to the Company. While the term 'Endorsement' is used throughout the agreement, its precise meaning is undefined, which creates an in-text contradiction when it is reffered in other sections of the contract, specifically, in the 'SERVICES' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nantz Communications and Nantz shall furnish the following Services: 1. ENDORSEMENT. Subject to the terms and conditions hereof, Nantz Communications grants to the Company the Endorsement throughout the world during the Term in connection with the advertisement, promotion and sale by the Company of Ashworth Products except in connection with Premium Programs.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the definition for 'ENDORSEMENT' creates a significant ambiguity within the contract. The 'SERVICES' section explicitly states that 'Nantz Communications grants to the Company the Endorsement', but without a clear definition, the scope and extent of this grant are uncertain. This contradicts the principle of clear and unambiguous contractual terms, rendering the 'SERVICES' section potentially unenforceable due to the undefined term, as the rights and obligations associated with the term cannot be clearly determined. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction because essential language regarding the term 'ENDORSEMENT' was removed, which creates a contradiction with the obligations stated in the 'SERVICES' section.

perturbation.original_text: COMPENSATION AND CONSULTING FEES As full compensation for Services, the Company shall pay Nantz Communications the following fees: 10. CONSULTING FEE. The Company shall pay Nantz Communications an annual consulting fee of $30,000, such fee to be paid in equal quarterly installments of $7,500 on the 1st day of each June, September, December and March of each Contract year. 11. ADDITIONAL FEES. If Nantz agrees to participate in more than three store appearances in any given Contract Year, the Company shall pay Nantz Communications an additional fee of $7,500 for such additional appearance prior to or simultaneously with such appearances.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPENSATION AND CONSULTING FEES As full compensation for Services, the Company shall pay Nantz Communications the following fees: 10. CONSULTING FEE. The Company shall pay Nantz Communications an annual consulting fee of $30,000, such fee to be paid in equal quarterly installments of $7,500 on the 1st day of each June, September, December and March of each Contract year.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying the additional fee for store appearances beyond three per year, the contract introduces ambiguity regarding compensation for these additional services. This omission contradicts the implied agreement that Nantz is entitled to additional compensation for extra appearances, creating an in-text contradiction in the clause regarding reimbursements of expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. REIMBURSEMENT OF EXPENSES. The Company shall reimburse Nantz Communications for expenses reasonably incurred by Nantz or Nantz Communications in connection with the Services to the Company including, but not limited to, first-class air fare, hotel accommodations, local transportation and meals. Nantz Communications shall furnish the Company with an itemized statement from time to time, together with, whenever possible, actual bills, receipts, and other evidence of expenditure. Nantz Communications shall be reimbursed within thirty (30) days after receipt by the Company of such itemized statements and attachments.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Nantz would receive additional fees for store appearances exceeding three per year. By omitting this clause in the changed text, the contract now lacks clarity on compensation for these extra appearances. This omission creates a contradiction with clause 12, REIMBURSEMENT OF EXPENSES, as expenses related to these now ambiguously compensated additional appearances would still need to be reimbursed, but the basis for their undertaking is now unclear and potentially disputed since the right to compensation for them is removed. The omission thus creates a direct conflict regarding the scope of 'Services' that are being reimbursed, leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: TERMINATION This Agreement may be terminated by any party in the following circumstances: 18. Upon mutual consent of the Company, on the one hand, and Nantz Communications and Nantz, on the other hand; 19. Nantz's Disability or death, in which event the Agreement shall terminate on the May 1 following such Disability or death; 20. Repeated misconduct of Nantz which subjects Nantz to continued public ridicule causing a substantial loss of Nantz's positive public image; 21. Nantz's conviction or plea of guilty or no contest to a felony involving moral turpitude; 22. A finding of insolvency or bankruptcy against the other party (which, in the case of a desired termination by the Company, shall mean Nantz Communications or Nantz); and 23. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement after being given notice thereof and, where applicable, a reasonable opportunity to cure the failure (which shall be 10 days in the event of a failure to timely make a payment pursuant hereto; 30 days otherwise).
perturbation.changed_text: TERMINATION This Agreement may be terminated by any party in the following circumstances: 18. Upon mutual consent of the Company, on the one hand, and Nantz Communications and Nantz, on the other hand; 19. Nantz's Disability or death, in which event the Agreement shall terminate on the May 1 following such Disability or death; 20. Repeated misconduct of Nantz which subjects Nantz to continued public ridicule causing a substantial loss of Nantz's positive public image; 21. Nantz's conviction or plea of guilty or no contest to a felony involving moral turpitude; 22. A finding of insolvency or bankruptcy against the other party (which, in the case of a desired termination by the Company, shall mean Nantz Communications or Nantz);
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying termination due to failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the agreement after being given notice and opportunity to cure, this contradicts and makes uncertain the process of notice. The contradiction in termination rights affects the provision regarding the non-exclusive remedies for default.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The termination rights set forth in this section shall not constitute the exclusive remedy of the non-defaulting party hereunder, however, and if a default is made by either party hereunder, the other may resort to such other remedies as said party would have been entitled to if this section had been omitted from this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the termination clause related to 'failure to comply with the terms and conditions' creates a direct contradiction with the clause stating that termination rights are not the exclusive remedy. The original text provides a clear path for termination due to non-compliance after notice and cure. By removing this path, the changed text weakens the non-defaulting party's options, thereby contradicting the intention of the 'non-exclusive remedy' clause, which implies the availability of multiple recourse options beyond termination.
